org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.base.Function;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.FluentIterable;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * A {@link WindowOperator} that also allows an {@link Evictor} to be used.\n *\n * <p>The {@code Evictor} is used to remove elements from a pane before/after the evaluation of\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} and after the window evaluation gets triggered by a {@link\n * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger}.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class EvictingWindowOperator<K, IN, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends WindowOperator<K, IN, Iterable<IN>, OUT, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // these fields are set by the API stream graph builder to configure the operator\n\n    private final Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?>\n            evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // the fields below are instantiated once the operator runs in the runtime\n\n    private transient EvictorContext evictorContext;\n\n    private transient InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>> evictingWindowState;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    public EvictingWindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<Iterable<IN>, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(\n                windowAssigner,\n                windowSerializer,\n                keySelector,\n                keySerializer,\n                null,\n                windowFunction,\n                trigger,\n                allowedLateness,\n                lateDataOutputTag);\n\n        this.evictor = checkNotNull(evictor);\n        this.evictingWindowStateDescriptor = checkNotNull(windowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentProcessingTime()) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                            + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // check if the window is already inactive\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code EvictorContext} is a utility for handling {@code Evictor} invocations. It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> getEvictor() {\n        return evictor;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked, rawtypes\")\n    public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n        return (StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?>)\n                evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n    }\n}\n","numLines":506,"startLine":1,"totalLines":506}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01YSQ145NKLABAgF1vV1Vktv","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good! I see that EvictingWindowOperator has the same structure and same logic for handling late elements. The late data side output logic is identical (lines 267-273).\n\nNow, let me think about what the bug might be. Looking at the code more carefully, I notice something: **the condition for side-outputting a late element only checks if `isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)`**\n\nBut what if an element:\n1. Is assigned to a window (non-empty `elementWindows`)\n2. The window is late, so it's retired and skipped\n3. The element is never added to any window, so `isSkippedElement` remains true\n4. The element is also late\n\nIn this case, the element should be side-output. But there's a problem: for merging windows, what if the element COULD have been merged into an existing non-late window, but the order of operations or the lateness check prevents this?\n\nActually, wait! I think I found the bug! Let me re-read the merging window code very carefully:\n\n```java\nfor (W window : elementWindows) {\n    W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, ...);    // drop if the window is already late\n    if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n        mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n        continue;\n    }    isSkippedElement = false;\n    // ... add element ...}\n```\n\nThe key insight is: if `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` is true, we retire the window and `continue`. This means we skip the rest of the loop, including the `isSkippedElement = false` assignment.\n\nBut we also don't break out of the loop! We continue to the next window in `elementWindows`. What if there are multiple windows assigned to the element, and all of them are late?\n\nIn that case, `isSkippedElement` would remain true. And then:\n```java\n\n\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe element gets side-output as late data. But here's the critical issue: **what if the element is assigned to windows, but the window merging logic determines that the merged window would be late and throws an exception instead of retiring it?** Looking at the merge function (lines 314-366 in WindowOperator), there's a possibility that merging windows could fail or behave unexpectedly when dealing with late windows.\n\nThe merge function checks if the resulting window timestamp plus allowed lateness is before the current watermark, and if so, throws an UnsupportedOperationException. This means if merging would create a window that's too late, it throws an exception, causing processElement() to fail entirely. The element never gets side-output—the job crashes instead of silently dropping it.\n\nLet me reconsider the actual problem. Re-reading the task description: \"the output tag is null at that point in the code path.\" This suggests the side output tag might not be properly available during the merge operation. When sideOutput() is called, it uses output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element), but if lateDataOutputTag is null, the code increments a counter instead. Perhaps for merging windows, the conditions for side-outputting aren't being satisfied, or the tag isn't being passed through correctly in that code path.\n\nThe scenario seems to involve an element arriving after the watermark that could extend an existing session window. The element gets incorrectly flagged as late even though it successfully merges. I should construct a test case: create an initial session window, advance the watermark, then send a late element capable of merging with that window to see if it's being side-output as late when it shouldn't be.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6972,"cache_read_input_tokens":56408,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":6972},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"55cf5916-8a32-4fa1-bda1-a9549487b8c8","uuid":"ed9efc9d-5b57-4496-8403-6f0f07e06798"}

425→            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n   426→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   427→        }\n   428→    }\n   429→\n   430→    /**\n   431→     * {@code EvictorContext} is a 81→/**\n    82→ * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n    83→ * Trigger}.\n    84→ *\n    85→ * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n    86→ * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n    87→ * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n    88→ * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n    89→ * WindowAssigner}.\n    90→ *\n    91→ * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n    92→ * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n    93→ * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n    94→ * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n    95→ *\n    96→ * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n    97→ * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n    98→ * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n    99→ * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n   100→ */\n   101→@Internal\n   102→public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n   103→        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n   104→        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n   105→\n   106→    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n   107→\n   108→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   109→    // Configuration values and user functions\n   110→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   111→\n   112→    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n   113→\n   114→    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n   115→\n   116→    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n   117→\n   118→    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n   119→\n   120→    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n   121→    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n   122→\n   123→    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n   124→    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n   125→\n   126→    /**\n   127→     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n   128→     *\n   129→     * <ul>\n   130→     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n   131→     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n   132→     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n   133→     * </ul>\n   134→     */\n   135→    protected final long allowedLateness;\n   136→\n   137→    /**\n   138→     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n   139→     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n   140→     * to this.\n   141→     */\n   142→    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n   143→\n   144→    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n   145→\n   146→    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n   147→\n   148→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   149→    // State that is not checkpointed\n   150→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   151→\n   152→    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n   153→    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n   154→\n   155→    /**\n   156→     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n   157→     * state is not mergeable.\n   158→     */\n   159→    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n   160→\n   161→    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n   162→    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n   163→\n   164→    /**\n   165→     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n   166→     * timestamp.\n   167→     */\n   168→    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n   169→\n   170→    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n   171→\n   172→    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n   173→\n   174→    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n   175→\n   176→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   177→    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n   178→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   179→\n   180→    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n   181→\n   182→    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n   183→    public WindowOperator(\n   184→            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n   185→            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n   186→            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n   187→            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n   188→            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n   189→            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n   190→            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n   191→            long allowedLateness,\n   192→            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n   193→\n   194→        super(windowFunction);\n   195→\n   196→        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n   197→\n   198→        checkArgument(\n   199→                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n   200→                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n   201→\n   202→        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n   203→        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n   204→        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n   205→        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n   206→        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n   207→        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n   208→        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n   209→        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n   210→    }\n   211→\n   212→    @Override\n   213→    public void setup(\n   214→            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n   215→            StreamConfig config,\n   216→            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n   217→        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n   218→    }\n   219→\n   220→    @Override\n   221→    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n   222→        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    @Override\n   226→    public void open() throws Exception {\n   227→        super.open();\n   228→\n   229→        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n   230→        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n   231→\n   232→        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n   233→\n   234→        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n   235→        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n   236→\n   237→        windowAssignerContext =\n   238→                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n   239→                    @Override\n   240→                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n   241→                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n   242→                    }\n   243→                };\n   244→\n   245→        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n   246→        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n   247→        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n   248→            windowState =\n   249→                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n   250→                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n   251→        }\n   252→\n   253→        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n   254→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   255→\n   256→            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n   257→            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n   258→                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n   259→            } else if (windowState != null) {\n   260→                throw new IllegalStateException(\n   261→                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n   262→            }\n   263→\n   264→            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   265→            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n   266→\n   267→            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n   268→                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n   269→                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n   270→\n   271→            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n   272→                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n   273→\n   274→            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n   275→            mergingSetsState =\n   276→                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n   277→                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n   278→                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n   279→            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n   280→        }\n   281→    }\n   282→\n   283→    @Override\n   284→    public void close() throws Exception {\n   285→        super.close();\n   286→        timestampedCollector = null;\n   287→        triggerContext = null;\n   288→        processContext = null;\n   289→        windowAssignerContext = null;\n   290→    }\n   291→\n   292→    @Override\n   293→    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n   294→        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n   295→                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n   296→                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n   297→\n   298→        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n   299→        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n   300→\n   301→        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n   302→\n   303→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   304→            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   305→\n   306→            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n   307→\n   308→                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n   309→                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n   310→                // actualWindow == window\n   311→                W actualWindow =\n   312→                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n   313→                                window,\n   314→                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n   315→                                    @Override\n   316→                                    public void merge(\n   317→                                            W mergeResult,\n   318→                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n   319→                                            W stateWindowResult,\n   320→                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n   321→                                            throws Exception {\n   322→\n   323→                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   324→                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n   325→                                                        <= internalTimerService\n   326→                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n   327→                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   328→                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n   329→                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n   330→                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n   331→                                                            + internalTimerService\n   332→                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n   333→                                                            + \" window: \"\n   334→                                                            + mergeResult);\n   335→                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   336→                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n   337→                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n   338→                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n   339→                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n   340→                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   341→                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n   342→                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n   343→                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n   344→                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n   345→                                                                + \" window: \"\n   346→                                                                + mergeResult);\n   347→                                            }\n   348→                                        }\n   349→\n   350→                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n   351→                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n   352→\n   353→                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n   354→\n   355→                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n   356→                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n   357→                                            triggerContext.clear();\n   358→                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n   359→                                        }\n   360→\n   361→                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n   362→                                        // state window\n   363→                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n   364→                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n   365→                                    }\n   366→                                });\n   367→\n   368→                // drop if the window is already late\n   369→                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n   370→                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n   371→                    continue;\n   372→                }\n   373→                isSkippedElement = false;\n   374→\n   375→                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n   376→                if (stateWindow == null) {\n   377→                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n   378→                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n   379→                }\n   380→\n   381→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   382→                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n   383→\n   384→                triggerContext.key = key;\n   385→                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n   386→\n   387→                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n   388→\n   389→                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   390→                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   391→                    if (contents != null) {\n   392→                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n   393→                    }\n   394→                }\n   395→\n   396→                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   397→                    windowState.clear();\n   398→                }\n   399→                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n   400→            }\n   401→\n   402→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   403→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   404→        } else {\n   405→            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n   406→\n   407→                // drop if the window is already late\n   408→                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n   409→                    continue;\n   410→                }\n   411→                isSkippedElement = false;\n   412→\n   413→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n   414→                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n   415→\n   416→                triggerContext.key = key;\n   417→                triggerContext.window = window;\n   418→\n   419→                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n   420→\n   421→                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   422→                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   423→                    if (contents != null) {\n   424→                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n   425→                    }\n   426→                }\n   427→\n   428→                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   429→                    windowState.clear();\n   430→                }\n   431→                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n   432→            }\n   433→        }\n   434→\n   435→        // side output input event if\n   436→        // element not handled by any window\n   437→        // late arriving tag has been set\n   438→        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n   439→        // element timestamp\n   440→        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n   441→            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n   442→                sideOutput(element);\n   443→            } else {\n   444→                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n   445→            }\n   446→        }\n   447→    }\n   448→\n   449→    @Override\n   450→    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n   451→        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n   452→        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n   453→\n   454→        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n   455→\n   456→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   457→            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   458→            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n   459→            if (stateWindow == null) {\n   460→                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n   461→                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n   462→                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n   463→                return;\n   464→            } else {\n   465→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   466→            }\n   467→        } else {\n   468→            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n   469→            mergingWindows = null;\n   470→        }\n   471→\n   472→        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n   473→\n   474→        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   475→            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   476→            if (contents != null) {\n   477→                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n   478→            }\n   479→        }\n   480→\n   481→        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   482→            windowState.clear();\n   483→        }\n   484→\n   485→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   486→                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n   487→            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n   488→        }\n   489→\n   490→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   491→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   492→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   493→        }\n   494→    }\n   495→\n   496→    @Override\n   497→    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n   498→        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n   499→        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n   500→\n   501→        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n   502→\n   503→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   504→            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   505→            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n   506→            if (stateWindow == null) {\n   507→                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n   508→                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n   509→                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n   510→                return;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   513→            }\n   514→        } else {\n   515→            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n   516→            mergingWindows = null;\n   517→        }\n   518→\n   519→        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n   520→\n   521→        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   522→            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   523→            if (contents != null) {\n   524→                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n   525→            }\n   526→        }\n   527→\n   528→        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   529→            windowState.clear();\n   530→        }\n   531→\n   532→        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   533→                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n   534→            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n   535→        }\n   536→\n   537→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   538→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   539→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   540→        }\n   541→    }\n   542→\n   543→    @Override\n   544→    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n   545→        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n   546→                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n   547→                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n   548→        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n   549→                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n   550→                .build();\n   551→    }\n   552→\n   553→    /**\n   554→     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n   555→     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n   556→     *\n   557→     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n   558→     * triggerContext object.\n   559→     */\n   560→    private void clearAllState(\n   561→            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n   562→            throws Exception {\n   563→        windowState.clear();\n   564→        triggerContext.clear();\n   565→        processContext.window = window;\n   566→        processContext.clear();\n   567→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   568→            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n   569→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   570→        }\n   571→    }\n   572→\n   573→    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n   574→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   575→    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n   576→        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n   577→        processContext.window = window;\n   578→        userFunction.process(\n   579→                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n   580→    }\n   581→\n   582→    /**\n   583→     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n   584→     *\n   585→     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n   586→     */\n   587→    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n   588→        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n   589→    }\n   590→\n   591→    /**\n   592→     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n   593→     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n   594→     *\n   595→     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n   596→     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n   597→     */\n   598→    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n   599→        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   600→        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n   601→                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n   602→        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n   603→    }\n   604→\n   605→    /**\n   606→     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n   607→     * the given window.\n   608→     */\n   609→    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n   610→        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   611→                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n   612→    }\n   613→\n   614→    /**\n   615→     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n   616→     *\n   617→     * @param element The element to check\n   618→     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n   619→     */\n   620→    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n   621→        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n   622→                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n   623→                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n   624→    }\n   625→\n   626→    /**\n   627→     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n   628→     *\n   629→     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n   630→     */\n   631→    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n   632→        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n   633→        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n   634→            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n   635→            return;\n   636→        }\n   637→\n   638→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   639→            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   640→        } else {\n   641→            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   642→        }\n   643→    }\n   644→\n   645→    /**\n   646→     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n   647→     *\n   648→     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n   649→     */\n   650→    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n   651→        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n   652→        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n   653→            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n   654→            return;\n   655→        }\n   656→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   657→            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   658→        } else {\n   659→            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   660→        }\n   661→    }\n   662→\n   663→    /**\n   664→     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n   665→     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n   666→     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n   667→     *\n   668→     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n   669→     */\n   670→    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n   671→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   672→            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n   673→            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   674→        } else {\n   675→            return window.maxTimestamp();\n   676→        }\n   677→    }\n   678→\n   679→    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n   680→    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n   681→        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n   682→    }\n   683→\n   684→    /**\n   685→     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n   686→     * state access for {@link\n   687→     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n   688→     */\n   689→    public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n   690→\n   691→        // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n   692→        // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n   693→        protected W window;\n   694→\n   695→        public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n   696→                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n   697→            super(\n   698→                    keyedStateBackend,\n   699→                    new SerializerFactory() {\n   700→                        @Override\n   701→                        public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n   702→                                TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n   703→                            return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n   704→                                    executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\n   705→                        }\n   706→                    });\n   707→        }\n   708→    }\n   709→\n   710→    /**\n   711→     * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\n   712→     */\n   713→    public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n   714→\n   715→        public MergingWindowStateStore(\n   716→                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n   717→            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n   718→        }\n   719→\n   720→        @Override\n   721→        public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n   722→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   723→                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n   724→        }\n   725→\n   726→        @Override\n   727→        public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n   728→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   729→                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n   730→        }\n   731→\n   732→        @Override\n   733→        public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n   734→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   735→                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n   736→        }\n   737→\n   738→        @Override\n   739→        public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\n   740→                AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\n   741→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   742→                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n   743→        }\n   744→\n   745→        @Override\n   746→        public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\n   747→ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01K2TqfQHKAPjA8mSKWpDjJn","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! Now I have identified the bug. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:\n\n## Summary of Findings\n\nI've conducted a thorough search of the Apache Flink codebase and found a **critical bug related to late data and side output handling in merging/session windows**.\n\n### The Bug\n\n**Location**: \n- `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` (lines 303-373)\n- `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java` (lines 123-192)\n\n**Bug Description**:\n\nIn the `processElement()` method, when handling **merging windows** (like session windows), late-arriving elements are not properly side-output to the late data tag when the merged window becomes late.\n\n**Detailed Issue**:\n\n1. **For Merging Windows** (lines 303-403 in WindowOperator.java):\n   - When an element arrives, it's assigned to windows via `elementWindows`\n   - For each window, `mergingWindows.addWindow()` is called with a merge function\n   - **The problem**: If the merge results in a window that is late (its `maxTimestamp + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark`), the code only:\n     - Retires the window (line 370)\n     - Continues to the next window (line 371)\n     - Sets `isSkippedElement = false` only if the window is NOT late (line 373)\n   \n   - **Critical Bug**: When a window merge causes a window to become late:\n     - The element is added to `mergingWindows` during the merge function execution\n     - The element is marked as skipped ONLY if ALL windows it was assigned to are processed (the check at line 373 is inside the loop)\n     - When `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` is true, `isSkippedElement` remains `true` even though the element was technically handled\n     - At the end (line 440), `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`, the element is side-output only if **both conditions are true**\n     - However, if the element was dropped because of a late merged window, it should be side-output even if it wasn't truly \"skipped\"\n\n2. **For Non-Merging Windows** (lines 405-432):\n   - The code correctly handles late elements by:\n     - Dropping the element if the window is late (line 408-409)\n     - Setting `isSkippedElement = false` only after successfully processing (line 411)\n     - This allows the final check at line 440 to properly side-output\n\n**The Core Problem**:\n\nIn merging windows, when a **merge causes a window to become late**:\n- The element is never added to the window state (because we continue at line 371)\n- But `isSkippedElement` remains `true` \n- The element is only side-output if it ALSO meets `isElementLate()` criteria based on its own timestamp\n- However, the element might not be \"late\" by its timestamp but the merged window it was assigned to is late\n- This means **late elements can be silently dropped without being side-output** in session/merging window scenarios\n\n### Evidence in Code\n\n**WindowOperator.java lines 303-373**:\n```java\nif (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n    MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n    \n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // ... merge happens ...\n        \n        // drop if the window is already late\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n            continue;  // <-- ELEMENT DROPPED WITHOUT BEING MARKED AS SKIPPED!\n        }\n        isSkippedElement = false;  // <-- ONLY SET IF WINDOW IS NOT LATE\n```\n\n**EvictingWindowOperator.java shows identical pattern** (lines 123-192)\n\n### Missing Comment/TODO\n\nThe code lacks any TODO or FIXME comment indicating awareness of this issue, but there IS evidence that the developers understood merging window late element handling can be problematic:\n\n**WindowOperatorContractTest.java line 1877-1878**:\n```java\n/**\n * A misbehaving {@code WindowAssigner} can cause a window to become late by merging if it moves\n * the end-of-window time before the watermark. This verifies that we don't allow that.\n */\n```\n\nThis test checks that we **reject** windows that become late by merging (throw an exception), but it doesn't address the scenario where:\n- An element arrives and is properly timestamped\n- The element is assigned to valid (non-late) windows\n- Those windows merge and the result becomes late\n- The element should be side-output but currently may not be\n\n### Impact\n\nThis bug affects:\n- **Session Windows** (which are merging windows)\n- **Any merging window implementation**\n- Scenarios where:\n  - Now I have identified the bug. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:\n\n## Summary of Findings\n\nI've conducted a thorough search of the Apache Flink codebase and found a **critical bug related to late data and side output handling in merging/session windows**.\n\n### The Bug\n\n**Location**: \n- `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` (lines 303-373)\n- `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java` (lines 123-192)\n\n**Bug Description**:\n\nIn the `processElement()` method, when handling **merging windows** (like session windows), late-arriving elements are not properly side-output to the late data tag when the merged window becomes late.\n\n**Detailed Issue**:\n\n1. **For Merging Windows** (lines 303-403 in WindowOperator.java):\n   - When an element arrives, it's assigned to windows via `elementWindows`\n   - For each window, `mergingWindows.addWindow()` is called with a merge function\n   - **The problem**: If the merge results in a window that is late (its `maxTimestamp + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark`), the code only:\n     - Retires the window (line 370)\n     - Continues to the next window (line 371)\n     - Sets `isSkippedElement = false` only if the window is NOT late (line 373)\n   \n   - **Critical Bug**: When a window merge causes a window to become late:\n     - The element is added to `mergingWindows` during the merge function execution\n     - The element is marked as skipped ONLY if ALL windows it was assigned to are processed (the check at line 373 is inside the loop)\n     - When `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` is true, `isSkippedElement` remains `true` even though the element was technically handled\n     - At the end (line 440), `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`, the element is side-output only if **both conditions are true**\n     - However, if the element was dropped because of a late merged window, it should be side-output even if it wasn't truly \"skipped\"\n\n2. **For Non-Merging Windows** (lines 405-432):\n   - The code correctly handles late elements by:\n     - Dropping the element if the window is late (line 408-409)\n     - Setting `isSkippedElement = false` only after successfully processing (line 411)\n     - This allows the final check at line 440 to properly side-output\n\n**The Core Problem**:\n\nIn merging windows, when a **merge causes a window to become late**:\n- The element is never added to the window state (because we continue at line 371)\n- But `isSkippedElement` remains `true` \n- The element is only side-output if it ALSO meets `isElementLate()` criteria based on its own timestamp\n- However, the element might not be \"late\" by its timestamp but the merged window it was assigned to is late\n- This means **late elements can be silently dropped without being side-output** in session/merging window scenarios\n\n### Evidence in Code\n\n**WindowOperator.java lines 303-373**:\n```java\nif (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n    MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n    \n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // ... merge happens ...\n        \n        // drop if the window is already late\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n            continue;  // <-- ELEMENT DROPPED WITHOUT BEING MARKED AS SKIPPED!\n        }\n        isSkippedElement = false;  // <-- ONLY SET IF WINDOW IS NOT LATE\n```\n\n**EvictingWindowOperator.java shows identical pattern** (lines 123-192)\n\n### Missing Comment/TODO\n\nThe code lacks any TODO or FIXME comment indicating awareness of this issue, but there IS evidence that the developers understood merging window late element handling can be problematic:\n\n**WindowOperatorContractTest.java line 1877-1878**:\n```java\n/**\n * A misbehaving {@code WindowAssigner} can cause a window to become late by merging if it moves\n * the end-of-window time before the watermark. This verifies that we don't allow that.\n */\n```\n\nThis test checks that we **reject** windows that become late by merging (throw an exception), but it doesn't address the scenario where:\n- An element arrives and is properly timestamped\n- The element is assigned to valid (non-late) windows\n- Those windows merge and the result becomes late\n- The element should be side-output but currently may not be\n\n### Impact\n\nThis bug affects:\n- **Session Windows** (which are merging windows)\n- **Any merging window implementation**\n- Scenarios where:\n  - `allowedLateness` is configured with a late data output tag\n  - An element with a valid timestamp arrives\n  - Windows it's assigned to merge, causing the result window to become late\n  - The element gets silently dropped instead of being side-output to the late data tag\n\n### Related Code Files\n\n1. `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` - Main bug location\n2. `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java` - Same bug in evicting variant\n3. `/workspace/flink-tests/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/test/windowing/sessionwindows/SessionWindowITCase.java` - Session window tests (lines 71-77 show late event handling)\n4. `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorContractTest.java` - Contract tests noting merging window issues (line 1877)"}],"totalDurationMs":50389,"totalTokens":63166,"totalToolUseCount":32,"usage":{"input_tokens":296,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1708,"cache_read_input_tokens":59668,"output_tokens":1494,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1708,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

In the WindowOperator.processElement() method, in the merging window case, there are TWO loops that could affect `isSkippedElement`:\n1. The main loop (lines 306-400) - sets `isSkippedElement = false` when window is NOT late\n2. After the loop - at line 440, we check `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`But wait, there's only one loop in the merging case. So what am I missing?\n\nActually, let me re-read the exact flow one more time, very carefully:\n\n```java\n// For merging windows (lines 303-403)\nif (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n    MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n    \n    for (W window : elementWindows) {  // Loop over assigned windows\n        W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, ...);  // Try to merge\n        \n        // drop if the window is already late\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {  // Check if merged window is late\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n            continue;  // Skip to next window\n        }\n        isSkippedElement = false;  // Mark as processed\n        \n        // ... add element to window ...\n    }\n    \n    mergingWindows.persist();\n}\n\n// Check if element was skipped AND element is late\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nSo here's the potential issue: session windows only return one window per element, so the loop executes once. If that single window is late, it gets skipped and `isSkippedElement` remains true. Then the final check catches it as a late element. But if the window isn't late, `isSkippedElement` gets set to false and the element is processed normally.\n\nThe real question is whether the bug lies elsewhere—perhaps in how the side output gets registered or handled in the base class rather than in WindowOperator itself.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2845,"cache_read_input_tokens":79271,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2845},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"55cf5916-8a32-4fa1-bda1-a9549487b8c8","uuid":"cbdcfb33-d0c8-441f-b51e-e59017a750ec"}

OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                create();\n    }\n\n    private static final TypeInformation<Tuple2<String, Integer>> STRING_INT_TUPLE =\n            TypeInformation.of(new TypeHint<Tuple2<String, Integer>>() {});\n\n    // For counting if close() is called the correct number of times on the SumReducer\n    private static AtomicInteger closeCalled = new AtomicInteger(0);\n\n    // late arriving event OutputTag<StreamRecord<IN>>\n    private static final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late-output\") {};\n\n    private void testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize), Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int
